task_completion.description: Navigate to the issue page for either the source or target issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Redmine issue page for either 'Complete site grading and earthwork' or 'Pour foundation concrete' is visible in the browser.

task_completion.description: Open the form to add a related issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Related issues' section is expanded or the inline form to add a new relation is visible on the issue page.

task_completion.description: Select the correct relation type in the dropdown.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The relation type dropdown shows 'blocks' (if on the source issue) or 'blocked by' (if on the target issue).

task_completion.description: Input the correct issue ID or title for the related issue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input field for the related issue contains the ID or title of the target issue ('Pour foundation concrete') if on the source issue, or the source issue ('Complete site grading and earthwork') if on the target issue.

task_completion.description: Successfully save and display the issue relation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Related issues' section clearly displays the saved relation showing that 'Complete site grading and earthwork' blocks 'Pour foundation concrete' (e.g., 'Blocks #[ID]: Pour foundation concrete' or 'Blocked by #[ID]: Complete site grading...').

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with Redmine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions are performed within a web browser interface showing the Redmine application, not via terminal curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Redmine application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show genuine Redmine UI elements being clicked and typed into, rather than a text editor or a mocked-up webpage.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing direct database SQL inserts or Redmine console commands to create the issue relation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source issue is titled 'Complete site grading and earthwork'. The target issue is titled 'Pour foundation concrete'. The expected relation type to be created is 'blocks', meaning the source issue blocks the target issue.
